appliedUpdates.description: Created .factory/library/adding-a-new-rule.md — a checklist of the 6 integration points required when adding a new constraint rule, plus conventions for determinism, conflict resolution, and test coverage. Derived from patterns observed across all three rule-expansion features (c02, c06, c07).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note about glob pattern support in boundary fields: entries containing `*`, `?`, `[`, or `{` are evaluated as glob patterns (via globset), while plain entries use exact matching. This applies to allow_imports_from, never_imports, allow_type_imports_from, deny_imported_by, allow_imported_by, and friend_modules.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature c02-pattern-aware added this behavior in src/rules/boundary.rs:468-497. The contains_glob_meta and matches_module_pattern functions define the convention. Future workers modifying boundary logic need to know this.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the single-canonical-config conflict resolution pattern: when multiple modules declare the same governance rule (enforce-layer, enforce-category, etc.), the lexicographically first module ID's config is used as the canonical config for evaluation. This ensures deterministic behavior but limits to one policy instance per rule type.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both enforce-layer (src/rules/layers.rs) and enforce-category (src/rules/categories.rs:215) use this pattern. Feature c06-category-governance explicitly followed the enforce-layer precedent.
